pattern: Because multiplying by a negative number flips the sign, the best product reaching a cell isn't always built from the best product reaching its neighbors; tracking both the running maximum and minimum product at every cell lets a negative-times-negative turn into the new best.
hints: A single running 'best product so far' isn't enough, because multiplying a very negative product by another negative cell value can produce the largest positive product of all.
At each cell, track two values: the largest and the smallest product of any path reaching it from the top-left.

When moving into a cell, the new max is the largest of (max-from-above, max-from-left) times the cell if the cell is non-negative, but if the cell is negative you instead want the smallest incoming product times it; compute all four combinations and take the true max and min.

edge_inputs.description: no path can avoid a negative product
a mix of signs where a double-negative path wins
a single column with an odd number of negatives, forcing a negative result
zeros on the only viable path should still count as non-negative

stress.description: random small grids near the maximum dimensions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 15)
    n = rng.randint(1, 15)
    grid = [[rng.randint(-4, 4) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
